user_prompt: We will work on `lib\FlyGuiViews\RecordingView`, I've created some files inside with names that indicate their purpose
`RecordingView.cpp` itself will be used in two ways, one is for a call/meeting recording over bluetooth, and the other is a local memo recording
For a call/meeting, the speaker button is available, and there is a "answer call" button available.

For a local memo recording, there is no speaker button, and also no "answer call", but there is a button that can change the memo type (for things like: todo, journal, idea, etc). Cycle through the `sprit_memotype_` sprites
Both of these modes use the same layout.

There is always a red border, use four filled rectangles for this, the border is 10 pixels thick, drawing a box starting from x=0 y=10 (because we still want to see the top bar, do not move the top bar) down to the bottom right corner

Along the bottom of the screen, we now have 300 pixel wide area to work with because of two 10px thick borders, this is divided into three 100x100 pixel buttons. These buttons are also linked to the dedicated buttons `BtnA` `BtnB` and `BtnC`.

`AudioDeviceButton` can either be for a mic or a speaker. For a mic button, touching it will activate the mic for push-to-talk, touching it again will cause it to go back to speaker mode. The mic icon will be fully bright when in mic mode, and faded, with a red X draw over it when not in mic mode. For a speaker button, it is faded when in mic mode, and not faded when in speaker mode. There is also a speaker muted mode that is the not-faded button but with a red X drawn over it. When in speaker mode, touching the speaker button toggles speaker mute.
The drawing of the red X is a shared feature, draw the `sprit_muted_x` over the center of the button, it's a square so don't need to care about opacity.

Another shared feature: The top row of pixels is a audio level meter reflecting the peak levels. The line grows from the center outwards. When the peak level is at saturation, the entire row of pixels is fully red. When the level is 0, no pixels show. A low level is a short line in the center that is white. The colour reflects the level, Red is fully saturated, white is low or 0, yellow is in the middle.

The first of the three buttons is the mic button `sprit_btn_mic`, then the speaker button `sprit_btn_speaker` (if required), the third one will be a exit button `sprit_btn_end` that ends the recording.
The first coordinate is x=10 y=130, the second is x=110 y=130, the third is x=210 y=130
`RecordingView` is the owner of the code that changes the state of the buttons, not the buttons themselves
For the answer call button or the memo type button, the coordinate is x=260 y=70, size is 50x50, use `sprit_pickup` and `sprit_pickupdown`
If in bluetooth mode, there will be a static non-interactive sprite at x=260 y=20 that is also 50x50, use `sprit_bluetooth_50`
The icon inside "answer call" button changes according to `PhoneUiState CallManager::uiState()`. use `sprit_pickup` and `sprit_pickupdown`

Starting from x=14 y=101, there will be several lines of text. The first line is the recording file name, this is static. The second line should show how long the recording has been going on for, which needs the `lib\FlyGui\FlyGuiText.cpp`, the third line also uses `FlyGuiText` and shows all `getCallerInfoAt` items, one item at a time, cycling to the next one every 3 seconds.
For all the functions that need to control (so I don't mean just using getters) other modules like AudioManager or AudioFileRecorder or BtManager or etc, use `RecordingViewCallbacks.cpp`
This is a long instruction, warn me about mistakes
agent_reply: Implemented the new `RecordingView` stack under `lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView`:
- Added mic/speaker/answer-call/memo-type button classes.
- Added Bluetooth vs memo modes with shared layout, red border, bottom button mapping, caller-info cycling, elapsed time, filename text, and center-out audio meter.
- Added `RecordingViewCallbacks.cpp` for external control of `AudioManager`, `AudioFileRecorder`, and `BtManager`.
- Replaced the old registered `ActiveRecordingView` with the new `RecordingView` in `src/all_init.cpp`.
- Wired main-screen memo/smartphone/laptop buttons in `src/main.cpp`.
- Fixed the view redraw path so clearing the frame forces all child sprites/text back onto the screen.

Warnings I’d flag: the audio meter is drawn at `y=20`, inside the top red border, because `y=10` conflicts with the required always-red border and the visible top bar. Also, changing memo type after recording has started currently changes the icon only; the file/type is chosen when recording begins.
Verified with:
```powershell
pio run -e m5stack-core2
Build passes. `src/version.c` was regenerated by the build.

user_prompt: please move the functionality of `bool setSpeakerMuted(bool muted)` into `lib\AudioManager\AudioManager.cpp`
agent_reply: Moved speaker mute ownership into `AudioManager`.
`AudioManager` now exposes:
- `setSpeakerMuted(bool muted)`
- `toggleSpeakerMuted()`
- `muteSpeaker()`
- `unmuteSpeaker()`
- `speakerMuted()`
The old `RecordingViewCallbacks::setSpeakerMuted()` / toggle / getter remain as thin wrappers, so the view code doesn’t need to know about `AudioManager` directly.
I also made `AudioManager::setVolume()` respect the muted state: if speaker is muted, a new nonzero volume is remembered as the restore volume while actual output stays at `kMinVolume`.
Verified with:
```powershell
pio run -e m5stack-core2
Build passes. `src/version.c` was regenerated by the build.

[171474][W][main.cpp:314] connect_to_bluetooth_host(): [main.cpp] Bluetooth connection start failed: Busy
[175186][W][main.cpp:472] onclick_bluetooth_host(): [main.cpp] could not start Bluetooth host connection for index=3
[176923][I][main.cpp:469] onclick_bluetooth_host(): [main.cpp] scroll bluetooth host selected: index=3
[176932][I][main.cpp:301] connect_to_bluetooth_host(): [main.cpp] connecting to Bluetooth host from Bluetooth host list: name=Frank's S24 Ultra bdaddr=58:79:E0:36:81:59
[176948][W][main.cpp:314] connect_to_bluetooth_host(): [main.cpp] Bluetooth connection start failed: Busy
I can't start a connection after exiting a recording
also I think the phone is doing some sort of auto reconnect after I stop the recording

also... `[ 31756][I][BluetoothManager.cpp:859] hfp_event(): [BtManager] hfp subscriber number: 16506913758 type=4` showed up and it showed up on the LCD screen, seeing my own phone number is not helpful, reject that particular message
agent_reply: Fixed the two concrete problems.

Bluetooth recording stop now disconnects HFP when the active `RecordingView` is in Bluetooth mode, so the next host selection should not hit `Busy` just because the old call/meeting session stayed connected.

I also added a guard in [main.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp>) so if Android has already reconnected the same host, selecting it starts a new recording without trying a second HFP connect.
And `ESP_HF_CLIENT_CNUM_EVT` still logs the subscriber number, but no longer adds it to `CallManager` caller info, so your own phone number should stop rotating onto the LCD.
Verified with:
```text
pio run -e m5stack-core2
Build passed.
Fixed the two concrete problems.

Bluetooth recording stop now disconnects HFP when the active `RecordingView` is in Bluetooth mode, so the next host selection should not hit `Busy` just because the old call/meeting session stayed connected.

I also added a guard in [main.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp>) so if Android has already reconnected the same host, selecting it starts a new recording without trying a second HFP connect.
And `ESP_HF_CLIENT_CNUM_EVT` still logs the subscriber number, but no longer adds it to `CallManager` caller info, so your own phone number should stop rotating onto the LCD.
Verified with:
```text
pio run -e m5stack-core2
Build passed.

commit_message: progress on bluetooth but cannot initiate connection, can handle incoming though

user_prompt: We have made a lot of progress, a lot of things are working the way I want
Walk through the documentation I wrote earlier, we might've deviated a lot. Warn me where the implementation does not match the written documentation, and tell me suggested changes
Note: I am thinking about making the device way more secure, and the `bluetooth.json` and `wifi.json` will eventually go away, so ignore things that involve that
agent_reply: **Findings**
1. **The documented file-transfer/filesystem design no longer matches the code.**

The docs still say Wi-Fi provides an FTP server, the app treats onboard flash and microSD as separate disks, and LittleFS is used: [README.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/README.md:7), [file-system-and-ftp-server-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/file-system-and-ftp-server-design.md:1).
Current code has:
- no FTP implementation beyond a readme in [lib/FtpServer/readme.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FtpServer/readme.md:1)
- a microSD-only HTTP file server in [WebServer.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebServer.cpp:376)
- that HTTP server is only visibly initialized by the webserver test, not normal firmware startup: [test_webserver.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_webserver.cpp:53)
- only microSD filesystem bring-up in [MicroSdCard.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/MicroSdCard/MicroSdCard.cpp:74)
Suggested change: decide the real transfer direction now.
- If HTTP/web UI is the intended path, update README and filesystem docs from “FTP + LittleFS + two disks” to “microSD file access over HTTP/web UI” and delete or mark FTP notes as obsolete.
- If FTP and onboard flash still matter, they are implementation gaps, not documentation details.
2. **Several documented Wi-Fi and UI workflows are still scaffolding.**

The docs describe AP mode, station selection, NTP sync, cloud upload selection/progress, file viewing, and RTC setting: [ui-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/ui-design.md:3), [wifi-workflow.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/wifi-workflow.md:9).
In the current main flow:
- selecting a configured station, AP, cloud upload, and NTP action only logs: [main.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:530)

- `WifiChooserView`, `WebActionView`, `ApModeView`, and `UploadProgressView` are empty shells: [WifiChooserView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/WifiChooserView.cpp:3), [WebActionView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/WebActionView.cpp:3), [ApModeView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/ApModeView.cpp:3), [UploadProgressView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/UploadProgressView.cpp:3)
- `FileListView` is also only a placeholder: [FileListView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/FileListView.cpp:3)
Suggested change: split docs into:
- **implemented now**
- **planned workflow**
That would avoid the docs reading like finished product behavior while these surfaces are still stubs.
3. **The call-recording UI behavior has drifted from the written UI contract.**

The docs say the user can change call volume, mute/unmute the mic, and see a 3-pixel tally border that becomes dotted when local mic is muted: [ui-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/ui-design.md:7). The Bluetooth workflow also says the recording screen shows mute status and output volume: [bluetooth-workflow.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/bluetooth-workflow.md:19).
Current recording UI:
- exposes push-to-talk mode switching and **speaker** mute in [RecordingView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingView.cpp:185)

- uses a fixed 10-pixel solid red frame in [RecordingView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingView.cpp:21) and [RecordingView.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingView.cpp:403)
- has mic mute support in `AudioManager`, but I did not find GUI wiring for it: [AudioManager.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1307)
- has volume APIs, but not a recording-view volume control: [AudioManager.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1231)
Suggested change: choose one UI model.
- If the device is primarily push-to-talk on internal hardware, rewrite the docs around P2T and speaker mute.
- If mic mute and volume are still required call controls, add those controls and make the tally border encode mic state as documented.
4. **Recorder performance/file-format docs are slightly ahead of current behavior.**

The file-format doc says new recordings should pre-allocate roughly 1 GB and truncate afterward: [recording-file-format.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/recording-file-format.md:7). The dual-core note also assumes pre-grown `.rec` files: [dual-core-load-distribution.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/dual-core-load-distribution.md:113).

Current code has preallocation implemented but disabled by default: [conf.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h:14), [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:418).

Also, the doc says packets have a header and payload; the current on-disk implementation writes a **fixed-size** `file_packet_t` for normal `.rec` packets, including a fixed payload array and `payload_length`: [defs.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/defs.h:31), [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:230).
Suggested change:
- update the format doc to explicitly say `.rec` packets are fixed-size today
- say preallocation is optional/compile-time unless you intend to enable it again
5. **The high-level audio/hardware docs overstate two areas.**

The high-level design says an external WM8960 or SGTL5000 codec may be enabled by build flag: [high-level-fw-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/high-level-fw-design.md:9). In the current audio manager, external I2S codec enable functions are explicit “not implemented yet” stubs: [AudioManager.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:626).

The same doc says CVSD local PCM is 8 kHz: [high-level-fw-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/high-level-fw-design.md:31). The implementation accepts 8 kHz HFP, but `AudioFifo` upsamples 8 kHz input into its 16 kHz internal representation, and recorder packets are labeled `*_16KHZ_MONO`: [AudioFifo.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFifo/AudioFifo.h:90), [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:315).
Suggested change:
- state “external codec path planned, internal M5Stack audio path implemented”
- clarify audio-rate layers:
- HFP CVSD ingress/egress can be 8 kHz
- recorder/internal FIFO normalizes to 16 kHz today
**Things that still match well**

- Bluetooth connection auto-starts a call recording after entering a connected/audio-available state, matching the Bluetooth workflow: [bluetooth-workflow.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/bluetooth-workflow.md:13), [main.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:166).

- The recording stop path can also disconnect Bluetooth: [bluetooth-workflow.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/bluetooth-workflow.md:17), [RecordingViewCallbacks.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingViewCallbacks.cpp:64).

- Wi-Fi activation shuts down Bluetooth/audio/recording first, which matches the high-level design: [high-level-fw-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/high-level-fw-design.md:17), [WifiManager.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.cpp:291).

- RTC-based recording naming and `.rec` output are in place: [high-level-fw-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/high-level-fw-design.md:21), [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:68).
- Top bar date/time and battery status are implemented: [ui-design.md](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/developer-docs/ui-design.md:16), [FlyGui.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp:630).
**Security Note**

Ignoring the eventual removal of `bluetooth.json` and `wifi.json` as requested: the recording format already has security hooks, but they are placeholders right now. `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY` adds nonce fields and leaves encryption/authentication as TODOs: [defs.h](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/defs.h:39), [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:206).
Before hardening transfer/config storage, I would write one short security design doc that states:
- what is encrypted at rest
- what authenticates a recording packet/file
- how keys are provisioned and recovered
- what local UI actions are allowed without trust/authentication
- whether the HTTP file surface survives at all
I did not count `bluetooth.json` or `wifi.json` mismatches in the findings. I also did not run builds or tests for this pass; this was a documentation-to-code review.

user_prompt: Hmmm I swear I had it set to connectable by default, I walked through the code and you are indeed correct, I am not sure what happened. I've made an edit to `init_bluetooth` to start it up as connectable, and also the closing of the pairing window. Double check that this change is valid
Once you are sure, execute the plan
No need to care about the filename, I'll deal with the metadata later
agent_reply: Your Bluetooth scan-mode change is valid: `ESP_BT_CONNECTABLE, ESP_BT_NON_DISCOVERABLE` is the right idle/default state if you want known hosts to connect while keeping normal discovery closed. Pairing still opens discoverability, and outbound connect/shutdown still make Bluetooth non-connectable where appropriate.
I executed the plan:
- Added memo-to-Bluetooth UI promotion in [RecordingView.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingView.cpp:137).

- Added audio routing promotion in [RecordingViewCallbacks.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/RecordingView/RecordingViewCallbacks.cpp:65), which unchokes mic-to-Bluetooth and keeps mic mode active.
- Added the all-init helper to force redraw when already on the recording view in [all_init.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp:219).

- Changed the pending Bluetooth recording path so an incoming Bluetooth connection during a memo recording promotes the active recording instead of starting a new file in [main.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:258).
`pio run -e thefly_bringuptest` passed. I also restored `src/version.c` after the build touched it.
